county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Jonesboro. Created 1858 from Fayette and Henry. Atlanta metropolitan county — Battle of Jonesborough (1864). Clayton County Historical Society. 'Gone With the Wind' connection (Jonesboro). Rapid post-WWII suburban growth. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Fayette County for pre-1858 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1858 to present. Continuous coverage.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1858. Pre-1952 marriages at county level; state registration after 1952.

source_profiles.source_name: Clayton County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, and appraisements from 1858.
Confederate service records, Union occupation records, pension applications. Georgia Confederate pension files at GA Archives.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Homerville. 1856 fire destroyed most early records. Created 1850 from Lowndes and Ware. Okefenokee border county. Very rural — timber and turpentine economy. Limited institutional resources. GA Archives microfilm for post-1856 records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1856 to present. Some gaps due to 1856 fire destroyed most early records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1856 fire destroyed most early records

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1856. Pre-1952 marriages at county level; state registration after 1952.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1856 fire destroyed most early records

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, and appraisements from 1856.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1856 fire destroyed most early records

county_metadata.record_losses: 1864 Civil War; courthouse occupied and records damaged
county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Marietta. 1864 Civil War — Kennesaw Mountain battlefield; courthouse occupied, records damaged. Marietta Museum of History and Cobb County genealogical collections. Kennesaw Mountain National Battlefield records. Extensive post-war industrialization records. Cobb County Genealogical Society very active. National Cemetery records at Marietta.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1864 to present. Some gaps due to 1864 Civil War; courthouse occupied and records damaged.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax digests listing enslaved persons by slaveholder; estate inventories with enslaved individuals named; bills of sale. Available on GA Archives microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Coverage varies; some records lost in fires

source_profiles.source_name: Creek Nation Records — Cobb County Area
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-removal Creek records including census rolls, treaty records, and land lottery entries. Creek removal completed by 1830.
Confederate service records, Union occupation records, pension applications. Georgia Confederate pension files at GA Archives.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1854 from Clinch, Irwin, Telfair, Ware.
county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Douglas. Created 1854 from Clinch, Irwin, Telfair, and Ware. Coffee County Historical Society. South GA wiregrass region. FamilySearch microfilm. Agricultural community. Check multiple parent counties for pre-1854 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1854 to present. Continuous coverage.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, and appraisements from 1854.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Moultrie. Created 1856 from Lowndes and Thomas. Colquitt County Historical Society. Agricultural county (tobacco, peanuts, cotton). Moultrie city records supplement county records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check parent counties for pre-1856 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1856 to present. Continuous coverage.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

county_metadata.record_losses: 1856 courthouse fire at Appling; records rebuilt
county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Evans (formerly Appling, old county seat). 1856 courthouse fire at Appling — records partially rebuilt. Created 1790 from Richmond. Columbia County Historical Society. Fort Gordon (now Fort Eisenhower) military records. Rapid growth area near Augusta. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1856 to present. Some gaps due to 1856 courthouse fire at Appling; records rebuilt.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax digests list heads of household, property, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax digests often survive when other records are lost — many at GA Archives on microfilm

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Adel. Created 1918 from Berrien — check Berrien County for pre-1918 records. Small south GA agricultural county. Cook County Historical Society. FamilySearch microfilm. Adel city records available.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1918 to present. Continuous coverage.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Newnan. Created 1826 from Creek Cession lands. Coweta County is named for the Coweta town of the Creek Confederacy. Male Academy Museum in Newnan. Significant Civil War activity. Textile mill records. Coweta County Genealogical Society. FamilySearch microfilm. Strong plantation records pre-1865.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1826 to present. Continuous coverage.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

Superior Court minutes include civil and criminal cases, name changes, naturalization, and guardianship records.
Tax digests listing enslaved persons by slaveholder; estate inventories with enslaved individuals named; bills of sale. Available on GA Archives microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax digests generally well preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Creek Nation Records — Coweta County Area
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-removal Creek records including census rolls, treaty records, and land lottery entries. Creek removal completed by 1830.

county_metadata.record_losses: Records damaged; some early records lost
county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Knoxville. Records damaged; some early records lost. Created 1822 from Houston. Small central GA county. Limited institutional resources. GA Archives microfilm essential. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Houston County for pre-1822 records.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Cordele. Created 1905 from Dooly. Crisp County Historical Society. Cordele is 'Watermelon Capital of the World.' Georgia Veterans State Park nearby. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Dooly County for pre-1905 records.
